title: Real-world prose: short narrative
description: Block 5 opener — sustained narrative paragraph at higher speed
text: The morning light slipped through the curtains and fell on the wooden desk. She poured herself a coffee, opened the laptop, and started writing the report that was due by noon. Outside, the city woke up.

tips.text: Now the two goals travel together: speed and accuracy, neither one bought at the cost of the other.
A good sentence already carries its own rhythm. Your job is to find it and ride it, not to fight it.

tips.target: The morning light slipped through the curtains and fell on the wooden desk. She poured herself a coffee,

tips.text: A comma is a breath, not a full stop. Let it set the tempo and keep your hands moving through it.

tips.target: opened the laptop, and started writing the report that was due by noon. Outside, the city woke up.

character_tips.anna: From here on, plain prose is the measure. Speed and accuracy climb side by side or not at all. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: This is the writing you will actually do. Type it the way you would read it aloud, calm and even. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: No symbols to dodge now, just a story. Let it run through your hands and don't look down. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: A well-made sentence has its own current. Find it, and the typing carries itself. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
